sourceMovie.title: Unsane
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Unsane works as a horror movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The horror and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Split
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Unsane clicked for you, Split (2017) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Unsane?
faq.answer: Try Split, Case 39, Haunt—each shares DNA with Unsane while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Unsane?
faq.answer: Unsane (2018) is best described as Horror, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Unsane worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this horror lane.
